title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, five-time Pro Bowl cornerback, announced his retirement after 13 NFL seasons at age 35.
Gilmore played for six teams, spending four years with the Patriots, and won Super Bowl LIII in 2018 with them.
He ended his career with 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions, earning a Defensive Player of the Year award.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle has started global layoffs as part of organizational changes, affecting roles in multiple countries.
No Oracle layoffs have been reported in Massachusetts as of April 2 under local WARN Act filings.
Analysts estimate up to 30,000 employees, about 18% of Oracle's workforce, could be affected.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signed a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, marking his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger recorded 42 tackles, two interceptions, and a sack in nine games with the Steelers last year.
The former Patriots captain played 17 games and made 109 tackles during the 2023 season with New England.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Byrnes family returned to Boston to find their truck stolen from Logan Airport’s central parking garage.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the truck theft, similar to another Logan Airport garage incident weeks earlier.
The stolen truck reportedly exited Logan Airport by piggybacking behind another vehicle and was tracked to New Jersey.

title: This registered nurse is running Boston for Special Olympics Massachusetts
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Gabby Peduto, a registered nurse from Wakefield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Special Olympics Massachusetts.
Peduto works with students with disabilities and is inspired by Special Olympics Massachusetts' mission of inclusion and empowerment.
The Boston Marathon run will help Peduto fundraise for Special Olympics Massachusetts, emphasizing athletes' capabilities over their diagnoses.

title: North Andover police investigate ‘unauthorized’ social media comment about Kelsey Fitzsimmons case
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover Police investigate unauthorized Facebook comment about former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons's civil litigation against the town.
Comment allegedly made by a former employee, who resigned in 2023 after racially inappropriate comments, has prompted an internal investigation.
Police department is auditing social media accounts and policies to prevent unauthorized access, involving consultations with legal and IT experts.

title: The Federal closes in Waltham after 7 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Federal, a seafood and steak restaurant in Waltham, closed after seven years due to rising costs.
Located at 74 Tower Rd, the restaurant was known for menu items like oysters and shellfish towers.
Despite its closure, The Federal's sister property, Stazione di Federal, will remain open and honor gift cards.

title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com offers a curated list of 16 new spring book releases, organized on a mood-based reading matrix.
Readers can sign up to receive a detailed book matrix and top four picks directly in their email inbox.
The matrix includes diverse book options ranging from fast-paced to slow-burning, and light to dark themes.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express will open a Centurion Lounge with an open-air terrace at Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
The 20,000 square-foot lounge will feature local artwork, premium food, WiFi, and Boston-inspired design.
Massport approved a $75 million budget and American Express will spend $391 million, including $10 million annual rent.

title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's sports bar at 96 Brookline Ave. near Fenway Park closed after eight successful years.
The closure announcement surprised many customers, with some thinking it was an April Fools’ joke.
West End Johnnie's, the sister location, remains open on Portland St. in Boston.
